<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-sequencer-api-v30-connecttosequencerchannelrequest">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.sequencer.api.v30.ConnectToSequencerChannelRequest</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">5 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">The sequencer channel service protocol consists of several messages which are sent as a request or a response (that is as a ConnectToSequencerChannelRequest or a ConnectToSequencerChannelResponse). The protocol transitions through several stages. - To bootstrap the channel, initialization messages between the sequencer channel client and service need to be exchanged, that are SequencerChannelMetadata and SequencerChannelConnectedToAllEndpoints messages. - To secure the channel, sequencer channel client member endpoints setup a session key with the SequencerChannelSessionKey and SequencerChannelSessionKeyAck messages. - To transfer data through the channel, members use opaque (encrypted) payload messages which the sequencer channel service forwards between channel member endpoints. Using payload messages channel client users can implement their own application protocol such as the Online Party Replication. Note that both, request and response need to implement SequencerChannelSessionKey and SequencerChannelSessionKeyAck messages because of the bidirectional gRPC streams. For example, when the sequencer channel service receives a SequencerChannelSessionKeyAck request message from a sequencer channel client, it needs to forward it as a SequencerChannelSessionKeyAck response message to the destination sequencer channel client.</p>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">metadata</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SequencerChannelMetadata</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Exactly the first message of the channel request stream must be SequencerChannelMetadata for use by the sequencer channel service to match channel endpoint members.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">session_key</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SequencerChannelSessionKey</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">SequencerChannelSessionKey and SequencerChannelSessionKeyAck are used to send and acknowledge an asymmetrically encrypted session key between the sequencer channel client endpoints. The SequencerChannelSessionKey is opaque to sequencer channel service.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">session_key_acknowledgement</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SequencerChannelSessionKeyAck</span>
      
    </div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">payload</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bytes</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Payload is opaque to the sequencer channel service and client.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">trace_context</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">TraceContext</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Canton trace context Required</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-sequencer-api-v30-sequencerchannelsessionkey">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">

    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">1 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">Public key encrypted session key to secure the communication between two members.</p>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">

    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The public key is determined by the timestamp as agreed by the connecting members beforehand. The timestamp is expected to originate from a recent topology transaction that has already taken effect; meaning it&#x27;s not a future timestamp. Required</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SequencerChannelConnectedToAllEndpoints</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Exactly the first message of the channel response stream must be SequencerChannelConnectedToAllEndpoints for use by the sequencer channel client to notify the request initiator that the channel is ready for use.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">

    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">SequencerChannelSessionKey and SequencerChannelSessionKeyAck are used to send and acknowledge an asymmetrically encrypted session key between the sequencer channel client endpoints. The SequencerChannelSessionKey is opaque to sequencer channel service.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">

      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">payload</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bytes</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Payload is opaque to the sequencer channel service and client.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">

      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">TraceContext</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Canton trace context Required</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">* Topology transaction collection used during bootstrapping of synchronizer nodes and on the admin API Please note that this message should not be in the protocol package, as it is not used on the protocol itself but on the admin apis. But as we can&#x27;t rename the package name due to backwards compatibility guarantees, we&#x27;ve moved the file out of the protocol.proto file, but kept the package name.</p>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">

      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">sequenced</code>

  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">

  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">

    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">* Versioned signed topology transactions serialized as byte-strings. Note that we use here the serialized version that contains the version number. Using the &quot;Versioned&quot; version directly here would create a circular dependency between the proto files. The only proper solution would be to move this into a third separate file with &quot;cross version data files&quot;, which we might do at some point.</div>

    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">An optional signature delegation where a long-term signing key authorizes a session signing key to sign on its behalf.</div>
    
  </div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">

      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bytes</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">ASN.1 + DER-encoding of X.509 SubjectPublicKeyInfo structure of the session public key: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5280#section-4.1. This key was used to produce the actual signature.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">session_key_spec</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SigningKeySpec</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The signing key spec for the session key.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">validity_period_from_inclusive</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">int64</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">A timestamp in microseconds of UTC time since Unix epoch that indicates when the session key is considered valid.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">validity_period_duration_seconds</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">uint32</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Duration in seconds indicating how long the session key remains valid. The validity period starts from `validity_period_from_inclusive` above.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">format</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SignatureFormat</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The format of the signature</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">signature</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bytes</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The signature of the combined hash of the session key fingerprint, validity period, and synchronizer id, by the long-term key. This signature authorizes the session key to act on behalf of the long-term key.</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">signing_algorithm_spec</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">SigningAlgorithmSpec</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">The signing algorithm specification used to produce this signature</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-protocol-v30-signedtopologytransaction">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.protocol.v30.SignedTopologyTransaction</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">4 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">transaction</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bytes</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">serialized topology transaction (protobuf bytestring)</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">signatures</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">repeated Signature</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">multiple signatures Either this field OR the multi_transaction_signatures field MUST contain at least one signature</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">proposal</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bool</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">if true, this transaction is just a proposal. this means that every signature is valid, but the signatures are insufficient to properly authorize the transaction. proposals are distributed via the topology channel too. proposals will be pruned automatically when the nodes are pruned TODO(#14045) implement pruning</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">multi_transaction_signatures</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">repeated MultiTransactionSignatures</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">If set, the transaction may be authorized by signing a hash computed from multiple transaction hashes This allows to effectively authorize multiple transactions with a single signature. Each item MUST contain the hash of this transaction Optional</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-protocol-v30-multitransactionsignatures">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.protocol.v30.MultiTransactionSignatures</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">2 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-description">Used in SignedTopologyTransaction to bundle together multiple transaction hashes Allows submitters to only sign a single combined hash to authorize multiple transactions at once The combined hash is computed from the transaction hashes</p>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">transaction_hashes</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">repeated bytes</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">List of topology transaction hashes. Required</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">signatures</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">repeated Signature</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Signatures on the combined hash computed over the transaction_hashes MUST contain at least one</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-sequencer-api-v30-subscriptionrequest">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.sequencer.api.v30.SubscriptionRequest</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">2 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">member</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">string</span>
      
    </div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">timestamp</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">int64</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Timestamp to start the subscription from (inclusive). Members can use the timestamp of the last event they have received to continue from there and compare the event, or can subscribe from previous event timestamp&#x27;s immediate successor to get the next event. To subscribe from the beginning, set the optional to None. If it refers to an event that has already been acknowledged, the sequencer may reject the request. In microseconds of UTC time since Unix epoch</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>

<div class="x2mdx-ref-schema" id="schema-com-digitalasset-canton-sequencer-api-v30-subscriptionresponse">
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-schema-head">
    <h3>com.digitalasset.canton.sequencer.api.v30.SubscriptionResponse</h3>
    
    <p class="x2mdx-ref-schema-summary">2 fields</p>
    
  </div>
  
  
<div class="x2mdx-ref-fields">
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">signed_sequenced_event</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">bytes</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">Contains a versioned SignedContent which itself contains a versioned SequencedEvent Serialized SignedContent[SequencedEvent[ClosedEnvelope]]</div>
    
  </div>
  
  <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-row">
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-main">
      <code class="x2mdx-ref-field-name">trace_context</code>
      <span class="x2mdx-ref-type-badge">TraceContext</span>
      
    </div>
    
    <div class="x2mdx-ref-field-description">optional, canton trace context DO NOT RENAME this field, as the ApiRequestLogger uses reflection to read this field. We typically use a header to transfer this information however streamed items do not have per item/message headers, so we instead in this instance set it in the body.</div>
    
  </div>
  
</div>

  
  
</div>
